decks.notes: The deck focuses on utilizing a variety of removal spells and disruptive creatures to control the board and outvalue the opponent. By efficiently removing threats with cards like Abrupt Decay and Fatal Push, while also pressuring their resources with cards like Graveyard Trespasser and Sheoldred, the Apocalypse, the deck aims to establish board dominance and grind out a victory in the mid to late game. The inclusion of hand disruption spells like Thoughtseize helps in disrupting the opponent's game plan and ensuring the deck can maintain control.

This UW Control deck focuses on controlling the game through a combination of counterspells, card draw, and removal spells. Beza, the Bounding Spring, Consult the Star Charts, and March of Otherworldly Light provide card advantage and selection, while Teferi, Hero of Dominaria and Dovin's Veto help control the board. Change the Equation and Ultima provide win conditions, while the rest of the deck aims to delay and disrupt the opponent's strategy. By balancing card advantage with control elements, this deck aims to grind out wins through attrition and careful decision-making.

This deck focuses on quickly getting Arclight Phoenix into the graveyard and then bringing it back to the battlefield multiple times in a single turn. It utilizes cheap and efficient spells like Fiery Impulse and Lightning Axe to both trigger the Phoenix's ability and control the opponent's board. The deck also includes card draw and selection spells like Opt and Consider to help find the pieces needed to set up the combo. Additionally, the deck contains a mix of evasion and disruption cards to keep the pressure on the opponent while protecting its own game plan.

The main concept of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive playstyle focused on green devotion cards. By utilizing cards like Nykthos, Shrine to Nyx and The Great Henge to generate mana and card advantage, while also deploying powerful threats like Craterhoof Behemoth and Emrakul, the Promised End. The deck aims to quickly build a board presence using creatures like Llanowar Elves and Badgermole Cub, while also disrupting the opponent's strategy with cards like Invasion of Ikoria and Fight Rigging.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly and efficiently fill up the graveyard with cards like [[Consider]], [[Opt]], and [[Treasure Cruise]] in order to bring back [[Arclight Phoenix]] from the graveyard as a powerful threat. It also includes various burn spells like [[Fiery Impulse]] and [[Lightning Axe]] to control the board and deal direct damage to the opponent. Additionally, cards like [[Picklock Prankster]] and [[Ledger Shredder]] provide card advantage and disruption to help maintain pressure on the opponent.

The main focus of this deck is to maximize green mana production and devotion to green to power up key cards such as Craterhoof Behemoth and The Great Henge. With a strong emphasis on aggro tactics, the deck utilizes efficient creatures like Badgermole Cub and Llanowar Elves to quickly build a board presence. Nature's Rhythm and Nykthos, Shrine to Nyx are key cards that help ramp up mana production, while cards like Invasion of Ikoria and Spider Manifestation provide additional support for the aggressive strategy. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm opponents with fast, powerful green creatures and enchantments.

This deck focuses on using a combination of hand disruption, removal spells, and creatures that can disrupt the opponent's game plan. It also utilizes mana acceleration to cast powerful threats like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse and Emeritus of Abundance. The deck aims to control the board and grind out incremental advantages over time, eventually overwhelming the opponent with its threats. The inclusion of cards like Boseiju, Who Endures and Culling Ritual provides additional utility and resilience against certain strategies. Overall, the deck excels at disrupting opponent's plans and gaining incremental advantages to secure victory.

The deck focuses on controlling the game through removal spells like Fatal Push and Abrupt Decay, while also utilizing graveyard interaction with cards like Graveyard Trespasser. It aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan with discard spells like Thoughtseize and establish board presence with creatures such as Badgermole Cub and Sentinel of the Nameless City. The inclusion of powerful creatures like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse and Emeritus of Abundance allows for strong late-game plays. The deck also includes land cards like Mutavault and Boseiju, Who Endures to provide additional utility and consistency in its control strategy.

The main idea of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and disruption while utilizing various land cards to assist in mana fixing and boosting the power of the creatures. The deck also includes powerful creatures with abilities that can help control the board and ultimately secure the win. Additionally, the inclusion of cards like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push adds another layer of disruption and removal to keep the opponent on their toes. Overall, the goal is to quickly build a threatening board presence and disrupt the opponent's strategy to secure victory.

This deck focuses on quickly pumping up creatures with auras like All That Glitters, Ethereal Armor, and Sentinel's Eyes to create powerful threats. It also includes cards like Gryff's Boon and Sheltered by Ghosts to protect and buff creatures even further. The deck aims to overwhelm opponents with aggressive creatures and auras to quickly take control of the game. Additionally, cards like Sram, Senior Edificer and Mox Amber help with card draw and mana acceleration to keep the pressure on opponents.

This deck focuses on assembling the combo of Greasefang and Parhelion II to overwhelm the opponent with powerful token creatures and control spells. The key strategy is to ramp up mana quickly with Cache Grab and Esika's Chariot, while disrupting the opponent's game plan with cards like Thoughtseize and Collective Brutality. Overlord of the Balemurk can provide additional support for the combo, and Witherbloom Command and Bitter Triumph offer flexibility in dealing with various threats. The deck aims to create a dominant board presence and secure victory through a combination of aggression and disruption.

The focus of this deck is on utilizing control elements to disrupt the opponent's strategy while slowly building up resources and establishing a strong board presence. The deck includes a mix of removal spells like Fatal Push and Abrupt Decay, hand disruption with cards like Thoughtseize and Duress, and powerful creatures like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse and Graveyard Trespasser. The deck aims to outvalue the opponent over time and grind out victories through attrition and resource management. The mana base is designed to support the deck's color requirements and ensure a consistent stream of resources.

The main idea of this deck is to put pressure on the opponent by playing fast and aggressive creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Kumano Faces Kakkazan. It also utilizes burn spells like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage to control the board and finish off opponents quickly. The inclusion of cards like Emberheart Challenger and Soul-Scar Mage helps to deal with bigger threats while maintaining a fast pace. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with consistent damage and efficient removal spells, ultimately securing a quick victory.

The primary focus of this Red Deck Wins aggro deck is to quickly build up a board presence and apply pressure to the opponent. With a low mana curve and efficient creatures like Emberheart Challenger and Monastery Swiftspear, combined with burn spells like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage, the deck aims to deal damage quickly and efficiently. The inclusion of cards like Soul-Scar Mage and Screaming Nemesis further enhances the deck's aggressive strategy, while utility lands like Den of the Bugbear and Ramunap Ruins provide additional ways to push damage through.

Utilizing disruptive spells like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push, along with aggressive creatures like Kaito, Bane of Nightmares and Moon-Circuit Hacker, this Dimir Aggro deck aims to apply pressure early and control the opponent's resources. Mutavault and Faerie Miscreant provide additional threats, while Floodpits Drowner and Mockingbird offer resilience and card advantage. The deck also includes versatile lands like Watery Grave and Urborg, Tomb of Yawgmoth to support its game plan. With a mix of aggression and disruption, this deck seeks to overwhelm opponents and secure victory in Pioneer 1 vs 1 matches.

The main focus of this aggressive red deck is to quickly pressure the opponent using fast, low-cost creatures and burn spells. Monastery Swiftspear, Soul-Scar Mage, and Emberheart Challenger are key threats that can deal damage efficiently. The deck also includes powerful burn spells like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage to control the board and finish off the opponent. Den of the Bugbear and Ramunap Ruins provide additional reach in the late game. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with a constant barrage of damage and close out the game before they can stabilize.
